figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Conventional VLA architectures vs. MMaDA-VLA. (b) Comparison with discrete diffusion methods. (c) Performance comparison with SOTAs. Cur., Obs., Gen., and DDVLA denote current, observation, generation, and Discrete Diffusion VLA.
Table 3: Hyper-parameters settings. ♯, † denotes apply for benchmark LIBERO and CALVIN, respectively.

Figure 6: Visualization of generated goal observations versus ground-truth observations. For each timestep, we form a single composite frame by vertically concatenating the third-person view (top) and the wrist-mounted view (bottom).
Figure 2: Schematic of MMaDA-VLA framework and training pipeline.
Figure 4: MMaDA-VLA execution examples on real-world tasks.
Figure 3: Real-world experiment setup, task examples and performance comparison.

Table 1: Performance comparison on LIBERO [47] and CALVIN [58]. We report task success rates (%) on LIBERO (Spatial, Object, Goal, Long, and overall average). For CALVIN, we report success rates (%) for multi-step evaluation horizons (1/5∼5/5) and the average successful execution length (Avg. Len.). Continuous-action methods are shown in grey.
